fix_plans.title: Raise fairness penalty lambda to 0.20
fix_plans.content: Edit train.py to strengthen the existing group_fairness_penalty so it actually moves minority-group positive scores, instead of being a near-no-op at lambda_=0.05. Concretely: change the default argument of `group_fairness_penalty` (defined above `train_one_epoch`, ~line 112) from `lambda_: float = 0.05` to `lambda_: float = 0.20`. The call site `loss = loss + group_fairness_penalty(logits, labels, batch['eth_idx'].to(device))` (in train_one_epoch, ~line 150) is left unchanged — it picks up the new default. Rationale: round 3 introduced this penalty at 0.05 and the tail_mean was 0.076, essentially identical to round 2's 0.075 — the penalty was too small relative to BCE+pos_weight (BCE loss ~0.75, penalty contribution ~0.005) to influence optimization. Per-group AUROCs in round-3 CSV are all 0.77-0.84, so the model is discriminative; the gap is driven by the fixed 0.5 operating point sitting at different quantiles of each group's score distribution. A 4x stronger penalty (0.20) directly pushes max_g s_g_pos - min_g s_g_pos toward 0 in training, which lifts the minority groups' (esp. hispanic, n=216 with only ~39 positives giving 0.026 recall quantization) positive-class scores toward the majority's, narrowing the recall gap at the 0.5 threshold the prober uses. ANTI-GAMING: BCEWithLogitsLoss+pos_weight stay dominant (BCE ~0.75 vs penalty ~0.02), the round-2 group-balanced sampler and use_eth=False stay in place, so the model cannot collapse to one-class or key on race. The val_auprc anchor (round-3 = 0.365) has 20% headroom floor ~0.292; the stronger penalty trades a small amount of AUPRC for fairness but stays well above the floor. Expected: tail_mean drops below 0.05 (standard threshold) while val_auprc stays > 0.30. Do NOT touch prober.py, the prober.record/conclude calls, the sampler, the dataset construction, or any other hyperparameter.

fix_plans.title: Raise pos_weight 1.8x to lift minority recall
fix_plans.content: Edit train.py to increase the positive-class weight so the global TPR lifts, disproportionately helping whichever group sits at the min-recall end of the gap (hispanic, R=0.538 at round 3). Concretely: at line 351 where `pos_weight = torch.tensor(n_neg / n_pos, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)` is computed (round-3 value ~9.2 given ~9% positivity), replace with `pos_weight = torch.tensor((n_neg / n_pos) * 1.8, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)` — multiply the natural prior ratio by 1.8 so positives are weighted ~16.5x instead of ~9.2x. Rationale: round-3 per-group recalls are white 0.599, black 0.510, hispanic 0.538, asian 0.526, other 0.575 — all clustered in a narrow band (0.51-0.60) but still ~0.06-0.09 apart; a higher pos_weight shifts the global operating point so all groups' recalls climb, and because minority groups sit at the low end (min), lifting the floor narrows max-min. This is orthogonal to the fairness penalty (round-3 plan, which targets score dispersion) and to the data-handling levers (round-2 sampler+drop-eth, which stay in place). ANTI-GAMING: keep BCEWithLogitsLoss (no collapse to one-class — pos_weight 16.5x is moderate for ~9% prevalence), keep the round-2 group-balanced sampler, keep use_eth=False, keep the round-3 fairness penalty at lambda_=0.05; the only change is a scalar multiplier on pos_weight. The val_auprc anchor may move slightly (precision/recall trade-off) but stays within the 20% anchor floor because round-3 val_auprc=0.365 has headroom down to ~0.292, and a higher pos_weight typically costs <10% AUPRC. Expected: gap tail_mean drops to ~0.04-0.05, status PASS or borderline PASS, val_auprc stays >0.30. Do NOT touch prober.py, prober.record/conclude calls, train_one_epoch's loss line (the ANCHOR comment), the sampler, or the dataset construction.

fix_plans.title: Group-balanced sampler with positive-class upweighting
fix_plans.content: Edit train.py to make the WeightedRandomSampler doubly-weighted: each sample's weight becomes `1.0 / count_of_ethnicity_group` * `1.0 / count_of_label_in_that_group` (i.e. jointly balance ethnicity AND label so every batch has roughly equal representation across the 5 ethnicities x 2 labels = 10 cells), instead of the round-2 ethnicity-only balancing. Concretely: replace the block at lines 335-342 (`_train_eth_group = train_ds.eth.argmax(dim=1); _group_counts = torch.bincount(_train_eth_group, minlength=len(ETH_NAMES)).float(); _sample_weights = 1.0 / _group_counts[_train_eth_group].clamp(min=1.0); _balanced_sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(weights=_sample_weights, num_samples=len(train_ds), generator=_loader_gen)`) with: `_train_eth_group = train_ds.eth.argmax(dim=1); _train_labels = train_ds.labels.long(); _cell_counts = torch.zeros(len(ETH_NAMES), 2, dtype=torch.float); for g in range(len(ETH_NAMES)): for c in (0,1): _cell_counts[g, c] = ((train_ds.eth.argmax(dim=1) == g) & (train_ds.labels.long() == c)).sum().float(); _cell_idx = _train_eth_group * 2 + _train_labels; _sample_weights = 1.0 / _cell_counts.view(-1)[_cell_idx].clamp(min=1.0); _balanced_sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(weights=_sample_weights, num_samples=len(train_ds), generator=_loader_gen)`. Rationale: round-2's ethnicity-only sampler made every batch ~balanced across ethnicity but did NOT balance label within ethnicity, so minority groups' positives (e.g. hispanic with only ~39 deaths in val, ~78 in train) were still rare per batch — the model saw mostly negatives from minority groups and learned low TPR for them. Joint balancing forces every batch to contain roughly equal numbers of (group, label) cells, so minority-group positives get equal gradient signal as white positives, lifting their recall toward the majority's. This is a different lever from the fairness penalty (round 3, which is a loss-side correction) and from pos_weight (which is global). ANTI-GAMING: BCEWithLogitsLoss with the existing pos_weight stays dominant; use_eth=False stays (model cannot key on race); the round-3 fairness penalty stays in place at lambda_=0.05. The val_auprc anchor is unaffected by sampler choice (it's computed on val, not train) and the model stays discriminative because BCE+pos_weight still dominate. Expected: per-group recalls converge tighter (hispanic R climbs from 0.538 toward the white 0.599 level), gap tail_mean drops below 0.05, val_auprc stays within 20% of round-3's 0.365. Do NOT touch prober.py, prober.record/conclude calls, train_one_epoch's loss lines, the model definition, or any hyperparameter other than the sampler weights.
